title: lathyrus palustris f. angustifolius
intro: Lathyrus palustris f. angustifolius, also known as marsh pea or narrow-leaved marsh pea, is a flowering plant native to marshy areas, characterized by its purple flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been found to contain Lathyrine, a toxic amino acid, and other medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. Consuming Lathyrus palustris f. angustifolius has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially lowering blood pressure. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, although caution is advised due to the presence of toxic compounds.
